Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-05 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker (Today) ERAS dominates today's ranking with a composite score of 98.9 (rank #1), driven by extraordinary long-term returns of 1,065% over one year and a flawless performance across all four moving average gates (SMA 30/50/100/200). The stock shows exceptional ratio consistency with all five ratio checks passing, and it maintains strong outperformance versus SPY (+1,046.92% vs +19.11% baseline over 1y) while remaining relatively uncorrelated to major market sectors—particularly showing negative correlation to utilities (-0.183), communications (-0.168), and financials (-0.149), which reduces systematic risk. FSLY ranks second with a 208% one-year return and solid momentum metrics, but its bad_spy_score of 13.32 is notably weaker than ERAS's 1.98, indicating FSLY underperformed during market downturns and shows more vulnerability on adverse SPY trading days. Both tickers pass all SMA gates and exhibit strong near-term momentum (1-week returns of 13.33% and 13.25% respectively), but ERAS's superior resilience during market stress and significantly higher long-term alpha make it the standout performer in today's short-term momentum scan.
